summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an electrifying ’88 show, highlighted by scorching Scarlet > Fire, soaring Watchtower and a transcendent Morning Dew.
review: The performance is widely praised for its high‑energy second set with standout moments on Fire, Watchtower and Morning Dew, while the first set is noted as a bit uneven. The recording, identified as a soundboard (sbd), is described as crisp, clear and solid throughout, with only minor early‑song hiccups. Overall fans consider this one of the stronger 1988 concerts.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both musical performance and recording quality; a few mixed comments lower the average but not enough to drop it below strong‑positive.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with solid board sound; a couple of reviewers note slight unevenness in the first song or two, but overall the recording is praised as great and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set is consistently lauded as a high‑water mark with powerful jams, while the first set receives mixed remarks for being uneven and having a weaker vocal delivery.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is repeatedly praised (shredding Watchtower, scorching Fire, amazing riffs), though his vocals are noted as weak on some songs.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh’s bass presence is highlighted on Gimme Some Lovin and the early Alabama take, with several reviewers noting he “steps up”.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums receive enthusiastic shout‑outs for being tight, full‑throttle and driving the fire of the second set.
